int.description: Arbitrary-precision signed integer type with no overflow

str.description: Immutable sequence of Unicode characters, the standard text type

bool.description: Boolean type with singletons True and False, subclass of int

list.description: Mutable ordered sequence supporting append, sort, and list comprehensions

dict.description: Mutable hash-based mapping preserving insertion order since Python 3.7

tuple.description: Immutable ordered sequence, used for fixed collections and multiple return values

set.description: Mutable unordered collection of unique hashable elements with set algebra operations

frozenset.description: Immutable version of set that can be used as dictionary keys and set elements

bytes.description: Immutable sequence of bytes for binary data and encoded text

None.description: The sole value of NoneType, used to represent the absence of a value

complex.description: Complex number with real and imaginary float parts, written as 3+4j

type.signature: class type(object) / class type(name, bases, dict)
type.description: The metaclass of all classes; type(x) returns the type of x

range.signature: class range(stop) / class range(start, stop, step=1)
range.description: Immutable sequence of numbers for efficient looping without storing all values

object.description: Base class for all classes in Python, provides default __repr__, __eq__, and __hash__
